stories: Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the 4th of April, 2026
Happy Easter weekend — I hope you've got something relaxing planned
Here's your Saturday fact: scientists have just discovered a brand-new species of termite deep in a South American rainforest canopy — and it looks strikingly like a miniature sperm whale
It's one of those discoveries that reminds you nature has a sense of humour
Your calendar is looking clear today — it's Saturday and Easter weekend, so enjoy the break
Over to Singapore, and there's a big move in the gold market
Bloomberg is reporting that Singapore is making a serious push to rival Hong Kong as a global gold trading hub, with the central bank offering gold storage facilities
It's a bold play in the commodities space
Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the 4th of April, 2026
Happy Easter weekend â Easter Sunday is tomorrow, so hopefully the day ahead is a good one
Here's your science fact to kick things off: there's a species of jellyfish called Turritopsis dohrnii that is technically biologically immortal
When it gets old or stressed, it can revert back to its juvenile polyp form and essentially start life all over again
Researchers are genuinely studying it for insights into aging â and yes, that makes a jellyfish one of the most scientifically intriguing animals on the planet
Your calendar is looking clear today â enjoy the Easter break
Over in Singapore, the Middle East conflict is escalating its footprint here
Separately, Singapore health authorities have confirmed that a mutated strain of mpox has spread locally for the first time
Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the 3rd of April, 2026
Here's a quick fact to start the day: honey never spoils
Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey inside Egyptian pharaohs' tombs, and it was still perfectly edible
Your calendar is looking clear today Ã¢ÂÂ a good chance to ease into the long Easter weekend ahead

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs is actively surveying Singaporeans based in the region, assessing who may need help getting out as airport closures escalate across parts of the Middle East
Meanwhile, MINDEF has confirmed a significant defence purchase Ã¢ÂÂ guided multiple launch rocket systems acquired from the United States
And if you're filling the tank this weekend, expect to pay more: petrol prices have been climbing steadily as the Strait of Hormuz crisis chokes global oil supply
